turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Tomás, you've been with Maple about a year. How's support been when you've needed it?

turns.speaker: Tomás
turns.text: Mixed. The onboarding specialist we got at the start was fantastic, genuinely. She set up our approval rules with me on a call and I felt taken care of. But once we were past onboarding, regular support got slow.

turns.speaker: Tomás
turns.text: First response is often a day or two, and it's clearly a different, more junior team. For an urgent close-the-books issue, two days is too long. I end up finding workarounds myself instead of waiting.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Switching gears, how well does Maple handle your reporting needs?

turns.speaker: Tomás
turns.text: That's actually my biggest gripe. The built-in reports are too basic. I can see spend by category, but I can't build a custom report comparing departments month over month without exporting everything to a spreadsheet.

turns.speaker: Tomás
turns.text: Constantly. And the export itself is limited, it caps at a date range and doesn't include the receipt links, so my CSV loses the audit trail. A proper custom report builder, and an export that keeps the receipt references, would save me hours every month.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Between support speed and reporting, which matters more to you?

turns.speaker: Tomás
turns.text: Reporting, by a lot. I can live with slower support if the product lets me self-serve the numbers I need. Right now the weak reporting is what makes my month-end painful.
